summary: First pass at untangling `src/melee/it/item.h`: the PR shrank `item.h`, moved many item prototypes into more specific item headers, added `it/inlines.h` and `ft/inlines.h` for inline helpers/macros, and converted numerous internal `item.c` helpers to `static`. It also cleaned include dependencies between fighter and item code, added Doxygen-style file notes for correctly placed item symbols, and made small naming/comment fixes such as `sfxID` to `sfx_id` in comments.
what_changed: `src/melee/it/item.h` was reduced from a large catch-all header to a smaller public item header with a modern include guard `MELEE_IT_ITEM_H`, a `@file` marker, and `@attention Symbols in this file are placed correctly.`

Large sets of item function declarations were moved out of `item.h` into `item2.h`, `code_80266F3C.h`, and `code_8027CF30.h`, with many declarations preserving or improving short comments about observed behavior.

New `src/melee/ft/inlines.h` was added and `fighter.h` now includes it. Fighter inline helpers/macros such as `getFighter`, `getFtAttrs`, `Fighter_OnItemPickup`, `Fighter_OnItemDrop`, `Fighter_OnKnockbackEnter`, `Fighter_UnsetCmdVar0`, `CLIFFCATCH_O`, and `CLIFFCATCH_U` were moved there.
`fighter.h` was simplified by removing inline helper bodies and keeping extern globals and fighter function declarations in the main fighter header.
`ft/types.h` no longer includes `melee/it/item2.h`, reducing a fighter type-header dependency on item declarations.

`item.c` internal helpers were converted to `static`, including setup/spawn/think helper functions such as `func_80267130`, `func_802674AC`, `func_802675A8`, `func_802676F4`, `func_8026784C`, `func_80267978`, `func_80267AA8`, `func_8026814C`, `func_802682F0`, `func_80268560`, `func_8026862C`, and several `lbl_80269...` item think callbacks.
`item.c` gained local static forward declarations for item think callbacks and other internal-only routines instead of exposing them through `item.h`.
`item2.c` now includes `code_80266F3C.h` and `code_8027CF30.h`, reflecting the split of item declarations.
`ftPurin/ftpurin.c` added `#include <melee/it/item2.h>` and `ftSamus/ftsamus.h` added `#include <melee/ft/inlines.h>` to satisfy the newly separated declarations.
`ft_unknown_006.c` only changed comments to use `sfx_id` consistently in the explanatory `Player_AdjustSFXIDForSizeModifier` notes.
smart_moves: Separated inline-heavy fighter helpers into `ft/inlines.h` instead of keeping them in `fighter.h`, which directly addressed include complexity.
Used `static` for item functions that appear to be translation-unit-local, reducing unnecessary global prototypes and making header boundaries more accurate.
Split item APIs by apparent object/source area rather than leaving all declarations in `item.h`; this makes future includes more targeted and searchable.
Kept small behavior comments on many unknown item functions while moving them, preserving decomp knowledge during the header cleanup.
Added explicit `@attention Symbols in this file are placed correctly.` markers to item headers, a useful signal for future symbol-placement work.
Updated includers such as Purin, Samus, and `item2.c` to include the narrower headers they actually need rather than relying on transitive inclusion.
decomp_lessons: Do not use major subsystem headers like `item.h` or `fighter.h` as dumping grounds for every known prototype; split declarations by source file or logical item module when evidence allows.
Inline helpers and matching-sensitive macros can live in a dedicated `inlines.h` to avoid forcing all consumers of the main type/function header to absorb many dependencies.
When moving declarations, update direct includes at call sites instead of preserving accidental transitive dependencies.
If a decompiled function is only used inside one C file, mark it `static` and keep any needed forward declaration local to that C file.
Preserve uncertainty in comments with question marks or short behavioral descriptions rather than overnaming unknown functions.
Changing headers may require small include fixes in unrelated character files because old monolithic headers often hid missing dependencies.
naming_conventions: Header guards use subsystem-qualified uppercase names such as `MELEE_IT_ITEM_H` and `MELEE_FT_INLINES_H` rather than older forms like `_item_h_`.
Item and fighter object parameters are named by role, for example `item_gobj`, `fighter_gobj`, `item_data`, `spawnItem`, `drop_gfx`, `drop_sfx`, and `sfx_id`.
Known-but-unnamed functions retain address-based names such as `func_8026B2B4` and `lbl_802693E4`, with comments documenting observed behavior.
Doxygen-style comments use `///` and occasionally references like `#func_8026B54C` or `#HSD_AObj`.
Inline accessors keep established names such as `getFighter`, `GetItemData`, `GetItemDirect`, `ftGetKind`, `ftGetAction`, and `getFtColl`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Kept separate inline variants such as `getFighterPlus`, documented as using more stack space, indicating inline shape can matter for matching.
Preserved `CLIFFCATCH_O` and `CLIFFCATCH_U` macros with comments distinguishing `fcmpo`-based and `fcmpu`-based facing-direction checks.
Moved inlines without rewriting their bodies, which helps preserve generated code while improving include structure.
Converted file-local helpers to `static`; this can align symbol visibility with original linkage and prevent unnecessary global declarations.
Retained comments that some item functions require `-g` compiler flag / Frank modifications to match, specifically around `func_8026B924` and `func_8026B960`.

review_feedback.applies_to: Removal of fighter inline helpers/macros from `fighter.h` and addition of `src/melee/ft/inlines.h`.

searchable_terms: Symbols in this file are placed correctly
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that continue `Fix item.h` or further split item headers.
Check whether declarations duplicated in `code_80266F3C.h` were deduplicated in a later cleanup.
Search for later movement or renaming of `it/inlines.h` and `ft/inlines.h` helpers.
Review subsequent item matching PRs that depend on functions made static in `item.c`.
Look for later convention changes around `GetItemData` versus `GetItemDirect` and lowercase parameter names like `sfx_id`.
